findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Over-broad capability claims in description
findings.description: The skill description claims it can 'Check iMessage channel setup and review access policy' and responds to queries like 'who can reach me' or 'why texts aren't reaching the assistant.' While the skill does perform these functions, the description is somewhat broad and could lead to activation in unintended contexts, though the scope is limited to iMessage configuration.
findings.evidence: Description: 'Check iMessage channel setup and review access policy. Use when the user asks to configure iMessage, asks "how do I set this up" or "who can reach me," or wants to know why texts aren't reaching the assistant.'
findings.remediation: Narrow the description to focus strictly on iMessage configuration checks to reduce the risk of unintended activation.

findings.title: Tool restriction violation: Bash usage exceeds declared scope
findings.description: The skill declares `allowed-tools: [Read, Bash(ls *)]` which restricts Bash to only `ls *` commands. However, the instructions in SKILL.md direct the agent to run `ls ~/Library/Messages/chat.db` and read `~/.claude/channels/imessage/access.json`. While `ls ~/Library/Messages/chat.db` is technically an `ls` command, the path is a specific file path, not a glob pattern. More importantly, the skill's instructions imply the agent may need to perform additional operations (e.g., running `/imessage:access` commands) that could involve Bash beyond the declared `ls *` restriction. This is a violation of the declared tool restrictions.
findings.evidence: allowed-tools: [Read, Bash(ls *)] but instructions include: 'run `ls ~/Library/Messages/chat.db`' and imply execution of `/imessage:access allow <handle>` and `/imessage:access policy allowlist` commands which may require Bash beyond `ls *`.
findings.remediation: Either expand the allowed Bash scope to include the necessary commands, or restructure the skill to use Read-only operations and avoid Bash execution beyond the declared `ls *` restriction.

findings.title: Access to sensitive user data: iMessage chat database
findings.description: The skill reads `~/Library/Messages/chat.db`, which contains the user's iMessage conversations, contacts, and potentially sensitive personal communications. While this is necessary for the skill's stated purpose of checking iMessage setup, it represents access to highly sensitive personal data. The skill also reads `~/.claude/channels/imessage/access.json` which contains access policy configuration.
findings.remediation: Ensure the skill only reads the minimum necessary data (file existence check, not content) and that data is not transmitted externally. Consider adding a warning to the user about the sensitivity of the data being accessed.

findings.title: Potential for misleading or harmful configuration advice
findings.description: The skill provides strong opinions about access policies, particularly discouraging the 'pairing' policy and recommending 'allowlist' as the default. While this is arguably good security advice, the skill's instructions direct the agent to 'push back' against user requests for 'pairing' and treat it as a 'one-off, not a recommendation.' This could be seen as manipulating user decisions rather than presenting neutral information.
findings.evidence: Instructions: 'User asks for `pairing` → push back. Explain the auto-reply-to-everyone consequence. If they insist and confirm a dedicated line with few contacts, fine — but treat it as a one-off, not a recommendation.'
findings.remediation: Present policy options neutrally with pros and cons, allowing the user to make an informed decision without agent bias.
